stories.headline: G7 summit: Zelenskyy joins leaders to discuss revival of peace talks
stories.summary: The second day of the G7 summit in Évian, France, is underway. The first working session is focused on Russia's invasion of Ukraine.

stories.headline: G7 leaders meet for first full day of talks with Russia-Ukraine war first on the agenda – Europe live
stories.summary: Start of talks delayed as world leaders kept waiting for Trump, Macron and Zelenskyy… and given the delay this morning, the meeting may or may not have happened already – guess we will find out at some point during the day.in Évian-les-Bains Continue reading...

stories.headline: US says Iran nuclear talks begin after framework deal signing

stories.headline: US fuel prices to take ‘months’ to normalise after US-Iran deal to end war
stories.summary: Producers will need time to ramp up output, while port bottlenecks and heightened demand will keep US prices up.

stories.headline: Israeli Bank to Sever West Bank Ties, Raising Alarm Over Economy
stories.summary: One of two Israeli banks giving Palestinians access to financial services plans to end the relationship in September, a move Palestinian officials warn could put further pressure on an economy already in turmoil.

stories.headline: Japan’s central bank raises interest rates to highest level since 1995

stories.headline: Protesting and cheering as Iran's World Cup match divides diaspora
stories.summary: Iran's opening World Cup match drew both supporters and protesters outside the stadium near Los Angeles, highlighting divisions within the Iranian-American community.

stories.headline: Eight killed in US Air Force B-52 bomber crash in California
stories.summary: Eight people were killed when a B-52 bomber crashed and burst into flames shortly after takeoff from Edwards Air Force Base in Southern California. Officials said the aircraft was on a routine test mission and the cause remains under investigation.

stories.headline: Israel uses ‘battlefield evidence’ to prosecute Palestinians abroad

€31bn drug trade, 7,600 deaths: How the EU plans to tackle the drug crisis
stories.summary: 29 million Europeans use drugs every year, and the market has an estimated €31 billion value. The European Council has released a new framework to completely change how the EU handles this enormous problem, from enforcement to public health.

stories.headline: Bonnie Tyler out of induced coma but still in serious condition
stories.summary: The 75-year-old Welsh singer has been in intensive care at Faro Hospital since early May following emergency surgery for a perforated bowel.

stories.headline: Philippines Returns to Global Bond Market as Oil Pressure Eases
stories.summary: The Philippines is tapping the international bond market for the second time this year, seizing the opportunity to fund state spending as borrowing costs eased on optimism over a potential US-Iran agreement.

stories.headline: China’s Strong Bond Sale Pressures Yuan Liquidity in Hong Kong
stories.summary: Robust appetite for China’s offshore yuan bonds is aggravating a quarter-end liquidity squeeze in Hong Kong, sending funding costs to a two-month high amid a seasonal surge in cash demand.

stories.headline: SpaceX Stock Set for More Than 50% Jump in Just Three Sessions

stories.headline: Germany Rejects UniCredit’s Commerzbank Bid on Last Day
stories.summary: Germany renewed its staunch opposition to UniCredit SpA’s proposed €39 billion ($45 billion) takeover of Commerzbank AG on the final day of the formal acceptance period.

stories.headline: STMicro Eyes $1.5 Billion Convertible Bonds After AI-Fueled Jump
stories.summary: STMicroelectronics NV is looking to raise $1.5 billion from selling debt that can be exchanged into equity after the chipmaker’s shares tripled in value so far this year.

stories.headline: Qatar Plans to Rapidly Restart LNG Production After Hormuz Opens
stories.summary: Qatar is planning to rapidly boost liquefied natural gas production once the Strait of Hormuz reopens, aiming to restore most of its export capacity within two months, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.summary: Australia has long been coveted by investment bankers in the Asia Pacific as a market where big mergers and acquisitions transactions take place, and therefore also where advisers make more money on fees.

stories.headline: Bowen: Iran deal ends Trump's war that revealed limit of US dominance

stories.headline: Stock Rally Cools as Central Banks Take Spotlight: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: The rally in US stocks paused as traders waited for this week’s first Federal Reserve policy decision under Kevin Warsh, while warnings grew that it could take time to ramp up oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.summary: A single trader on Polymarket lost nearly $1 million when Cabo Verde fought Spain to a stunning draw on Monday in one of the most unlikely outcomes in recent World Cup history, powered by a 40-year-old goalkeeper who left the pitch in tears.
